items.title: Lok Sabha Fails to Pass Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill, 2026 on Women’s Reservation
items.whyInNews: On April 2026, the Lok Sabha failed to pass the Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill, 2026, securing only 298 votes against the required 352 (two-thirds majority), leading to withdrawal of the Delimitation Bill, 2026 and Union Territories Laws (Amendment) Bill, 2026.
items.summary: The Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill, 2026 aimed to fast-track 33% women’s reservation by enabling delimitation based on the 2011 Census. The bill failed in the Lok Sabha with 298 votes in favour out of a required 352, triggering withdrawal of related bills. The failure has sparked political debate over federalism, representation, and timing of women’s reservation implementation.
keyPoints: Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill, 2026 received 298 votes but needed 352 for passage
Bill aimed to implement 33% women’s reservation through delimitation using 2011 Census
Failure led to withdrawal of Delimitation Bill, 2026 and UT Laws (Amendment) Bill, 2026
Debate emerged over federalism and North-South representation imbalance
importantPoints: Article 368: Constitutional amendment requires special majority (2/3rd present and voting + majority of total strength)
106th Constitutional Amendment Act, 2023 (Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam) provides 33% reservation for women
Delimitation freeze based on 1971 Census extended by 84th Amendment (2001) till post-2026 Census
Lok Sabha current strength considered: 528; required votes: 352; secured: 298
Issue impacts federal balance and future political representation in India
items.examRelevance: Important for SSC Polity under Parliament, Constitutional Amendments, Articles (368, 81, 82), and current affairs related to women’s reservation and delimitation

items.title: RELIEF Scheme Expanded with ₹497 Crore Outlay to Support Exports Amid West Asia Disruptions
items.whyInNews: In 2026, the Government of India expanded the Resilience & Logistics Intervention for Export Facilitation (RELIEF) Scheme in response to geopolitical tensions and maritime disruptions in West Asia, enhancing support for exporters.
items.summary: The RELIEF Scheme, launched under the Export Promotion Mission with a ₹497 crore outlay by the Ministry of Commerce and Industry, provides financial and logistical support to Indian exporters facing maritime risks. It covers exports to West Asia and Gulf countries including UAE, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and newly added Egypt and Jordan. The scheme is implemented by ECGC and offers up to 100% risk coverage for war-related losses and up to 95% coverage for shipments during disruption periods.
keyPoints: RELIEF Scheme launched under Export Promotion Mission with ₹497 crore outlay
Covers exports to West Asia and Gulf countries including UAE, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Egypt and Jordan
Provides up to 100% loss coverage for war-related risks and up to 95% for shipments during disruption
Implemented by Export Credit Guarantee Corporation of India (ECGC)
importantPoints: RELIEF stands for Resilience & Logistics Intervention for Export Facilitation
Launched by Ministry of Commerce and Industry to support exporters during geopolitical disruptions
ECGC (Export Credit Guarantee Corporation of India) handles insurance, claims and risk coverage
Focus on MSME exporters with insurance cap of ₹50 lakh under Component 3
Important for maintaining export competitiveness and supply chain resilience in global trade
items.examRelevance: Important for SSC Economy covering government schemes, export promotion policies, fiscal support measures and institutions like ECGC
